title: Peer-professional first-person account: schizophrenia from the inside--phenomenology and the integration of causes and meanings.
finding_one_line: A psychologist who himself became psychotic narrates his own recovery and the integration of medication, insight therapy and a changed social world.
verbatim_quote: I stepped off that vehicle later in West London with a radically different and, as far as I am concerned, 'corrected' brain from the one I had when I stepped on it.
abstract_sentence_recovery: qualities of experiential life were not merely causally impotent responses to brain processes but themselves inducing of critical decisions and of outlook on life that played a large part in the eventuation of, and recovery from, the psychotic state.
claims_it_can_support: A published, citable first-person voice on living with and recovering from schizophrenia
Recovery is possible and can be narrated by someone who experienced psychosis
transcription_note: Body quote from open-access PMC full text (PMC2632294); verify against source PDF before print. abstract_sentence_recovery is verbatim from Europe PMC core.

title: A systematic review and meta-analysis of recovery in schizophrenia.
finding_one_line: Pooling 50 studies, ~1 in 7 (13.5%) met a combined clinical+social recovery criterion sustained >=2 years, and the rate has not improved over decades. CONTESTED (see Lally 2017).

numbers_it_licenses.what_it_is: median proportion meeting combined clinical+social recovery criteria
numbers_it_licenses.abstract_sentence: The median proportion (25%-75% quantiles) who met our recovery criteria was 13.5% (8.1%-20.0%).
Based on the best available data, approximately, 1 in 7 individuals with schizophrenia met our criteria for recovery.
Despite major changes in treatment options in recent decades, the proportion of recovered cases has not increased.

claims_it_can_support: Headline recovery ~13.5% (1 in 7) for chronic/mixed samples
Recovery rates flat over decades
contested_note: CONTESTED, strict criterion, chronic samples; first-episode cohorts report higher. Report the range.

title: Remission and recovery from first-episode psychosis in adults: systematic review and meta-analysis of long-term outcome studies.
finding_one_line: In first-episode psychosis cohorts, pooled remission was 58% and recovery 38%, far higher than chronic-sample estimates, and outcomes did not progressively deteriorate.

numbers_it_licenses.what_it_is: pooled remission rate in first-episode psychosis
numbers_it_licenses.abstract_sentence: The pooled rate of remission among 12301 individuals with FEP was 58% (60 studies, mean follow-up 5.5 years).

numbers_it_licenses.what_it_is: pooled recovery prevalence in first-episode psychosis
numbers_it_licenses.abstract_sentence: The pooled prevalence of recovery among 9642 individuals with FEP was 38% (35 studies, mean follow-up 7.2 years).

claims_it_can_support: Recovery figure is definition/sample-dependent (chronic ~13.5% vs first-episode ~38%)
Progressive-deterioration model not typical
contested_note: This IS the contest source. Pair explicitly with Jaaskelainen 2013.

title: Supported employment for people with severe mental illness: systematic review and meta-analysis of the international evidence.
finding_one_line: IPS supported employment more than doubles competitive-employment rates vs traditional vocational rehabilitation, regardless of local economy.

numbers_it_licenses.what_it_is: pooled RR for competitive employment, IPS vs traditional vocational rehab
numbers_it_licenses.abstract_sentence: The overall pooled risk ratio for competitive employment using IPS compared with traditional vocational rehabilitation was 2.40 (95% CI 1.99-2.90).

claims_it_can_support: IPS roughly doubles competitive employment vs usual services

title: The effectiveness of supported employment for people with severe mental illness: a randomised controlled trial.
finding_one_line: EQOLISE RCT (6 European centres, n=312): 55% of IPS patients worked at least one day vs 28% with standard vocational services.

numbers_it_licenses.what_it_is: proportion working at least 1 day: IPS vs traditional vocational services
numbers_it_licenses.abstract_sentence: 85 (55%) patients assigned to IPS working for at least 1 day compared with 43 (28%) patients assigned to vocational services (difference 26.9%, 95% CI 16.4-37.4).

claims_it_can_support: Absolute employment rates for IPS vs usual care (~55% vs ~28%)

title: The population impact of severe mental illness on violent crime.
finding_one_line: Swedish national registers: only ~5% of violent crime is attributable to severe mental illness, patients commit roughly 1 in 20 violent crimes.

numbers_it_licenses.what_it_is: population-attributable risk fraction of SMI for violent crime
numbers_it_licenses.abstract_sentence: Overall, the population-attributable risk fraction of patients was 5%, suggesting that patients with severe mental illness commit one in 20 violent crimes.

claims_it_can_support: ~95% of violent crime NOT attributable to severe mental illness
Corrects the 'dangerous' stereotype with population data
contested_note: Anti-stigma correction. Absolute risk small even where relative risk elevated; most risk concentrated with substance misuse.

title: Perpetration of violence, violent victimization, and severe mental illness: balancing public health concerns.
finding_one_line: People with severe mental illness are more often victims of violence than perpetrators; victimization is the greater public-health concern.

numbers_it_licenses.what_it_is: prevalence of perpetrating vs being violently victimized (outpatients)
numbers_it_licenses.abstract_sentence: Among outpatients, 2% to 13% had perpetrated violence in the past six months to three years, compared with 20% to 34% who had been violently victimized.

claims_it_can_support: Victimisation rates far exceed perpetration rates for people with SMI

title: Years of potential life lost and life expectancy in schizophrenia: a systematic review and meta-analysis.
finding_one_line: Schizophrenia associated with ~14.5 years of potential life lost and life expectancy ~64.7 years; the gap has not narrowed over time.

numbers_it_licenses.what_it_is: weighted-average years of potential life lost
numbers_it_licenses.abstract_sentence: Schizophrenia was associated with a weighted average of 14.5 years of potential life lost (95% CI 11.2-17.8), and was higher for men than women.
The overall weighted average life expectancy was 64.7 years (95% CI 61.1-71.3), and was lower for men than women.

claims_it_can_support: ~14.5-year life-expectancy/YPLL deficit
Mortality gap substantial, not narrowing

title: Prevalence, incidence and mortality from cardiovascular disease in patients with pooled and specific severe mental illness: a large-scale meta-analysis of 3,211,768 patients and 113,383,368 controls.
finding_one_line: Severe mental illness carries ~10% CVD prevalence and substantially raised hazard of CVD death (HR 1.85), driven partly by antipsychotic use and obesity.

numbers_it_licenses.what_it_is: hazard ratio for CVD-related death, SMI vs controls
numbers_it_licenses.abstract_sentence: The incidence was also higher for ... CVD-related death (HR=1.85, 95% CI: 1.53-2.24).

numbers_it_licenses.what_it_is: pooled CVD prevalence in SMI patients
numbers_it_licenses.abstract_sentence: The pooled CVD prevalence in SMI patients (mean age 50 years) was 9.9% (95% CI: 7.4-13.3).

claims_it_can_support: CVD is a major driver of the mortality gap
Antipsychotic use and elevated BMI are modifiable contributors

title: Premature Mortality Among Adults With Schizophrenia in the United States.
finding_one_line: US cohort (N=1,138,853): schizophrenia carries 3.7x all-cause mortality; CVD is the single leading cause; accidents kill more than twice as many as suicide.

numbers_it_licenses.what_it_is: standardized mortality ratio vs general population
numbers_it_licenses.abstract_sentence: Adults with schizophrenia were more than 3.5 times (all-cause SMR, 3.7; 95% CI, 3.7-3.7) as likely to die in the follow-up period as were adults in the general population.
Accidental deaths (119.7 per 100,000 person-years) accounted for more than twice as many deaths as suicide (52.0 per 100,000 person-years).

claims_it_can_support: Mortality gap driven mainly by physical disease (CVD), not suicide alone
CVD single largest cause; suicide a minority of excess deaths
contested_note: Corrects assumption that suicide is the main killer.

title: The social determinants of mental health and disorder: evidence, prevention and recommendations.
finding_one_line: World Psychiatry review mapping causal evidence that structural social determinants (poverty, marginalization, disadvantage) shape mental-health outcomes and recovery.

numbers_it_licenses.what_it_is: causal link between social determinants and outcomes
numbers_it_licenses.abstract_sentence: we first map out the literature that supports a causal link between social determinants and later mental health outcomes.

claims_it_can_support: Housing, income, poverty causally track outcome and recovery (framing only)
contested_note: Framing source only, qualitative claim, not a numeric statistic.

title: Caregiver burden and the associated factors in the family caregivers of patients with schizophrenia.
finding_one_line: Among 215 family caregivers, 38.2% perceived severe burden, predicted by income, job loss from caregiving, illness duration and caregiving frequency.

numbers_it_licenses.what_it_is: proportion of family caregivers perceiving severe burden (Zarit)
numbers_it_licenses.abstract_sentence: Family caregivers of patients with schizophrenia reported a high level of caregiving burden, with 38.2% of the caregivers perceiving severe burden relating to their role.

claims_it_can_support: A substantial minority (~38%) of carers experience severe burden (one 215-carer sample)
contested_note: Single-country cross-sectional; illustrative, not global prevalence. Pair with Peng 2022.

title: Disease-Related Risk Factors for Caregiver Burden among Family Caregivers of Persons with Schizophrenia: A Systematic Review and Meta-Analysis.
journal: International Journal of Environmental Research and Public Health
finding_one_line: Meta-analysis of 34 studies: heavier caregiver burden is significantly associated with more severe symptoms, greater psychopathology, worse functioning and longer illness duration.

numbers_it_licenses.abstract_sentence: a significant association between a heavier burden and disease-related risk factors, including more severe symptoms, greater general psychopathology, greater severity of functional impairment, and longer duration of illness.

claims_it_can_support: Carer burden rises with symptom severity, functional impairment, illness duration
T1 backbone; pair with Rahmani 2022

title: First Person Account From a Person in Jail With Schizoaffective Disorder.
finding_one_line: First-person narrative of living with schizoaffective disorder while incarcerated, fear, voices, daily exhaustion. Alternate/backup FPA for P5.
verbatim_quote: It is exhausting to live like that and always be looking around and be prepared.
claims_it_can_support: Alternate first-person voice on daily lived experience
Intersection of SMI with social disadvantage
transcription_note: Body quote from open-access PMC full text (PMC8084429); verify against source PDF before print.
